children.heading: Procurement by Sergeant at Arms of Senate in places designated by Senator; places subject to use; lease of office space
children.label: a Procurement by Sergeant at Arms of Senate in places designated by Senator; places subject to use; lease of office space

children.text: The Sergeant at Arms of the Senate shall secure for each Senator office space suitable for the Senator’s official use in places designated by the Senator in the State he represents. That space shall be secured in post offices or other Federal buildings at such places. In the event suitable office space is not available in post offices or other Federal buildings, the Sergeant at Arms shall secure other office space in those places.

The Senator may lease, on behalf of the United States Senate, the office space so secured for a term not extending beyond the term of office which he is serving on the first day of such lease, except that, in the case of a Senator whose term of office is expiring and who has been elected for another term, such lease may extend until the end of the term for which he has been so elected. Each such lease shall contain a provision permitting its cancellation upon sixty days written notice by the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate, in the event of the death or resignation of the Senator. A copy of each such lease shall be furnished to the Sergeant at Arms. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to require the Sergeant at Arms to enter into or execute any lease for or on behalf of a Senator.

children.heading: Maximum amount of aggregate square feet for each Senator
children.label: b Maximum amount of aggregate square feet for each Senator
A
children.text: 5,000 square feet if the population of the State of the Senator is less than 3,000,000;

children.label: B
children.text: 5,200 square feet if such population is 3,000,000 but less than 4,000,000;

children.label: C
children.text: 5,400 square feet if such population is 4,000,000 but less than 5,000,000;

children.label: D
children.text: 5,800 square feet if such population is 5,000,000 but less than 7,000,000;

children.label: E
children.text: 6,200 square feet if such population is 7,000,000 but less than 9,000,000;

children.label: F
children.text: 6,400 square feet if such population is 9,000,000 but less than 10,000,000;

children.label: G
children.text: 6,600 square feet if such population is 10,000,000 but less than 11,000,000;

children.label: H
children.text: 6,800 square feet if such population is 11,000,000 but less than 12,000,000;

children.label: I
children.text: 7,000 square feet if such population is 12,000,000 but less than 13,000,000;

children.label: J
children.text: 7,400 square feet if such population is 13,000,000 but less than 15,000,000;

children.label: K
children.text: 7,800 square feet if such population is 15,000,000 but less than 17,000,000; or

children.label: L
children.text: 8,200 square feet if such population is 17,000,000 or more.

children.label: A
children.text: of a kind authorized by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, which shall include an information technology security closet and a secure lobby or reception area; and

children.label: B
children.text: approved by the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate.

children.heading: Maximum annual rental rate; maximum aggregate amount for acquisition of furniture, equipment, and other office furnishings
children.label: c Maximum annual rental rate; maximum aggregate amount for acquisition of furniture, equipment, and other office furnishings
A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the maximum annual rate that may be paid for the rental of an office secured for a Senator not in a post office or other Federal building shall not exceed the highest rate per square foot charged Federal agencies on the first day of the lease of such office by the Administrator of General Services, based upon a 100 percent building quality rating, for office space located in the place in which the Senator’s office is located, multiplied by the number of square feet contained in that office used by the Senator and his employees to perform their duties.

children.label: I
children.text: of a kind authorized by the Committee on Rules and Administration of the Senate, which shall include guard services, access control, and facility monitoring; and

children.label: II
children.text: approved by the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such costs are itemized separately in a manner approved by the Sergeant at Arms and Doorkeeper of the Senate.

The aggregate amount that may be paid for the acquisition of furniture, equipment, and other office furnishings heretofore provided by the Administrator of General Services for one or more offices secured for the Senator is $40,000 if the aggregate square feet of office space is not in excess of 5,000 square feet. Such amount is increased by $1,000 for each authorized additional incremental increase in office space of 200 square feet. Effective beginning with the 106th Congress, the aggregate amount in effect under this paragraph for any Congress shall be increased by the inflation adjustment factor for the calendar year in which the Congress begins. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the inflation adjustment factor for any calendar year is a fraction the numerator of which is the implicit price deflator for the gross domestic product as computed and published by the Department of Commerce for the preceding calendar year and the denominator of which is such deflator for the calendar year 1998.

children.heading: Senators subject to maximum amount of aggregate square feet and maximum annual rental rate
children.label: d Senators subject to maximum amount of aggregate square feet and maximum annual rental rate
A
children.text: the applicable square footage limitation of such subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: the total square footage of those offices that the Senator has on such date and which are continuously maintained in the same buildings in which such offices were located on such date.

children.label: A
children.text: that Senator continuously serves as a Senator; and

children.label: B
children.text: that office is maintained in the same building in which it was located on such date and contains not more than the same number of square feet it contained on such date.

Subject to the provisions of paragraphs (2), (3), (4), and (5), a Senator may lease one mobile office for use only in the State he represents and the contingent fund of the Senate is available for the rental payments (including by way of reimbursement) made under such lease together with the actual nonpersonnel cost of operating such mobile office. The term of any such lease shall not exceed 3 years. A copy of each such lease shall be furnished to the Sergeant at Arms of the Senate.

The maximum aggregate annual rental payments and operating costs (except furniture, equipment, and furnishings) that may be paid to a Senator under paragraph (1) shall not at any time exceed an amount determined by multiplying (A) the highest applicable rate per square foot charged Federal agencies by the Administrator of General Services in the State which that Senator represents, based upon a 100 percent building quality rating, by (B) the maximum aggregate square feet of office space to which that Senator is entitled under subsection (b) reduced by the number of square feet contained in offices secured for that Senator under subsection (a) and used by that Senator and his employees to perform their duties.

children.label: A
children.text: Liability insurance in the amount of $1,000,000 shall be provided with respect to the operation and use of such mobile office.

children.label: B
children.text: Either of the following inscriptions shall be clearly visible on three sides of such mobile office in letters not less than three inches high:“UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT VEHICLE“FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”;Or“MOBILE OFFICE OF SENATOR _____“FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY”.

No payment shall be made under paragraph (1) for rental payments and operating costs of a mobile office of a Senator which are attributable to or incurred during the 60-day period ending with the date of any primary or general election (whether regular, special, or runoff) in which that Senator is a candidate for public office, unless his candidacy in such election is uncontested.
Payment under paragraph (1) shall be made on a monthly basis and shall be paid upon vouchers approved by the Sergeant at Arms of the Senate.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: g Effective date
children.text: This section is effective on and after .July 1, 1974
